data.title: MDU Resources prices public offering of 10,152,284 shares of common stock at $19.70 per share.
data.description: BISMARCK, North Dakota, Dec. 4, 2025 – MDU Resources Group, Inc. disclosed a recent move to execute an underwritten public offering comprising 10,152,284 shares of its common stock. The per-share value has been set at $19.70 for public purchase. The offering remains subject to specific conditions before completion.

data.title: SEC warns ETF issuers against excessive leverage, halts filings proposing 3-5x leverage on assets.
data.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission has put a stop to the submission of multiple ETF applications that suggested a leveraging of three to five times on the asset being tracked.

data.title: Trident Digital sponsors Singapore Comic Con 2025 with enhanced digital engagement and promotions for attendees.
data.description: Tridentity will provide upgraded digital interaction, special offers, and on-site events at the premier pop culture convention in Southeast Asia. The convention is expected to attract over 100,000 attendees, with Tridentity aiming to engage with at least 50% of them through their digital platform. They plan to offer exclusive promotions to enhance the experience for attendees and set up interactive exhibits to further boost engagement. This initiative is part of Tridentity's strategy to solidify its presence in the pop culture market and strengthen connections with fans in the region.

data.title: SEALSQ invests in EeroQ for quantum chip design using electrons on helium, advancing $35M SEALQUANTUM.com investment in USA.
data.description: SEALSQ Corp (NASDAQ: LAES), a company specializing in Semiconductors, PKI, and Post-Quantum technology products, disclosed a strategic investment in EeroQ, a U.S.-based quantum chip design company pioneering electron-on-helium quantum computer technology. The investment is part of SEALSQ’s Quantum Made in USA initiative to bolster the U.S. and Europe's position in quantum-resistant technologies. The company recently launched SEALQUANTUM.com with a $35 million investment focus on quantum computing startups. Notably, SEALSQ has already invested in ColibriTD for quantum-as-a-service solutions and acquired French firm IC’ALPS. The EeroQ investment marks the first U.S. investment in line with the Quantum Made in USA strategy.
Vivoryon Therapeutics N.V. unveils Q3 2025 financial report and business updates.
Addex Therapeutics announces Q3 2025 financial results and corporate update.

data.title: Tecan proposes changes to Board at the 2026 AGM.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, which is an increase from the earlier forecast of 4.9%. This growth is attributed to the speedy recovery in advanced economies such as the United States, where the GDP is anticipated to expand by 6.2%. However, emerging markets and developing economies are forecasted to see a growth rate of 6.4%, lower than the previous estimate of 6.9%. The global trade volume is expected to increase by 8.4%, surpassing the 7.2% growth forecast from earlier this year. This surge is primarily due to the significant bounce back in trade activity, specifically in Asia where the goods trade volume is projected to rise by 14%. The robust recovery in global trade is a key driver behind the improved economic outlook for the year.

data.title: HiroPharmaConsulting (R) obtains patent for AI-enabled reliability assurance method for pharmacovigilance systems.
data.description: In Osaka, Japan, on December 4, 2025, HiroPharmaConsulting (R) Co., Ltd revealed the acquisition of a patent for a groundbreaking Good Pharmacovigilance Practice (GVP) Computerized System Validation (CSV) process. This cutting-edge methodology is specifically engineered to safeguard the dependability of pharmacovigilance systems.

data.title: Vietnamese brands Lacàph and BOLD84 changing global perception of Vietnamese coffee; gaining recognition on world stage.
data.description: In Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam on December 4, 2025, Lacàph Coffees and BOLD84 Vietnamese Coffee, brands owned by Collective Company Limited, are reshaping the global perception of Vietnamese coffee. These two brands were honored at prestigious international events, solidifying their position in the worldwide market.

data.title: Polymarket launching US app for waitlisted users post CFTC approval. Selected users get early access to bet on sports contracts.
data.description: Certain individuals residing in the United States will be granted early access to a predictive platform where they can wager on sports event contracts. This platform has been approved for a relaunch by a financial regulator. The users in the US would be among the initial batch to utilize this platform for predictive sports betting.

data.title: Analysts predict Bitcoin to bounce back with signs of stabilization. Bitfinex notes extreme deleveraging supporting a possible rise.
data.description: Bitfinex has suggested that Bitcoin's stability and potential for upward movement may be supported by factors such as "extreme deleveraging" and other key indicators. This assessment comes amidst fluctuating market conditions with Bitcoin currently priced at approximately [current price] and showing signs of resilience. The platform's analysis underscores the importance of monitoring these factors closely to gauge Bitcoin's future trajectory in the volatile cryptocurrency market.
Bernstein’s modeling says… Huawei will take 50% of China’s AI accelerator market next year.

data.title: Ukrainian authorities detain 2 in crypto extortion killing; Europe sees 30% of 70 wrench attacks this year amid rising crypto violence.
data.description: More than 20 out of approximately 70 wrench attacks reported this year have taken place in Europe, representing over 30% of the total incidents. The continent is experiencing a surge in crypto-fueled violence, with an increasing number of attacks involving the use of wrenches.

data.title: Check Point Software raised $1.75 billion through a private offering of 0.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2030, increased from $1.5 billion initially planned.
data.description: TEL AVIV, Israel, Dec. 03, 2025—Cybersecurity leader Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (Nasdaq: CHKP) revealed the pricing of $1.75 billion in convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A of the Securities Act of 1933. This amount was increased from the initial $1.5 billion announced. As part of this, the initial purchasers of the notes have been granted an option to buy an additional $250 million within a 13-day period post issuance. The settlement for the sale to the initial purchasers is anticipated on December 8, 2025, contingent on standard closing conditions.
Sources say the BOJ is likely to raise rates in December, with the government set to tolerate the move.

data.title: Startup Fin, led by ex-Citadel engineers, secures $17M to develop stablecoin payments app for global transactions.
data.description: Two former Citadel engineers have launched Fin, a stablecoin app for cross-border payments. The startup secured funding to develop the app amid growing interest from banks and fintech companies in digital assets.

data.title: BlackRock CEO Fink labels Bitcoin 'asset of fear,' shifts stance on crypto after 8 years with Coinbase CEO.
data.description: During a recent event, Larry Fink, the CEO of BlackRock, discussed the evolution of the company's perspective on cryptocurrency over the past eight years. Fink shared the stage with Brian Armstrong, the CEO of Coinbase. The discussion shed light on BlackRock's changing attitudes towards digital assets and blockchain technology.
Marathon says unexpected flaring has occurred at its refinery in Wilmington, California.
UK new car demand is projected to slip by around 1% in November ahead of the budget’s planned taxation on electric vehicles, SMMT preliminary data shows.

data.title: Chip used in Solana phones susceptible to unstoppable attack, as Ledger gained full control using electromagnetic pulses.
data.description: A recent report reveals that through the use of electromagnetic pulses, a Ledger was successfully compromised, allowing for complete control over a smartphone's chip. The incident highlights the vulnerability of devices to such attacks, sparking concerns over security. The exploit sheds light on potential threats posed by advanced hacking techniques, emphasizing the need for robust security measures. This development serves as a cautionary tale, underscoring the importance of safeguarding sensitive information stored on electronic devices.
